# HubEx UI — AI reference

## Purpose

HubEx UI is the design system for HubEx plugins: a React component library
(`@cvetkov_es/react`), its stylesheet (`@cvetkov_es/css`), and the design tokens that
back both (`@cvetkov_es/tokens`). If you are an AI building UI for a HubEx plugin,
this file is the single reference for what exists and how to use it correctly
— read it before hand-rolling any button, input, table, modal, badge, etc.
Every control below reproduces an existing pattern from the HubEx product; a
hand-rolled equivalent will visually and behaviorally diverge from the rest
of the system.

## Install

```bash
pnpm add @cvetkov_es/react @cvetkov_es/css
```

Both packages are required: `@cvetkov_es/react` ships components with **no**
CSS of their own (by design — this preserves tree-shaking, see Rules), and
`@cvetkov_es/css` ships the stylesheet those components' class names point at.

**Critical setup — do this once, at your app's entry point:**

```ts
// main.tsx / index.tsx — top level, once
import "@cvetkov_es/css";
```

This single import loads every `.hx-*` class and every `--hx-*` CSS custom
property for the whole app. Do **not** import `@cvetkov_es/css` more than once,
and do not import it from inside a component file — it belongs at the entry
point only.

**Icons:** the `Icon` component renders glyphs from the Material Icons
webfont by name (e.g. `<Icon name="home" />`) — it ships no font files and no
SVGs. Your app must load the Material Icons font itself and alias it to the
font-family `"material"` (e.g. Google's hosted webfont via a `<link>` tag, or
a self-hosted `@font-face` under that family name). If the font isn't loaded,
`Icon` renders the glyph name as plain text instead of the icon.

## Design tokens

Every token below is a real CSS custom property defined in
`@cvetkov_es/tokens` and loaded globally once you `import "@cvetkov_es/css"`. **For any
custom UI, use these variables (or the `.hx-*` classes below) — never
hardcode a hex value, a font stack, a radius, or a spacing number.**

| Variable | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `--hx-color-text` | `#1F1F1F` | primary text |
| `--hx-color-text-muted` | `#777777` | secondary/help text |
| `--hx-color-text-disabled` | `#999999` | disabled text |
| `--hx-color-brand` | `#32B4EB` | brand accent (primary buttons, focus, active states) |
| `--hx-color-brand-strong` | `#348AEE` | brand accent, stronger (tag text on brand-tint) |
| `--hx-color-brand-tint` | `#E8F7FD` | brand accent, tinted background |
| `--hx-color-danger` | `#ED1940` | destructive/error |
| `--hx-color-success` | `#3BA55D` | success |
| `--hx-color-warning` | `#F9C901` | warning |
| `--hx-color-border` | `#EEEEEE` | hairline borders |
| `--hx-color-border-strong` | `#DADADA` | input/control borders |
| `--hx-color-surface` | `#FFFFFF` | base surface (cards, inputs, popovers) |
| `--hx-color-surface-alt` | `#FEFEFE` | alternate surface |
| `--hx-color-surface-sunken` | `#F8F8F8` | sunken surface (table head/row hover) |
| `--hx-color-on-accent` | `#FFFFFF` | text/icon color on top of a colored surface |
| `--hx-font-family` | `Roboto, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", sans-serif` | the only font stack to use |
| `--hx-font-size-sm` | `11px` | small text (labels, hints) |
| `--hx-font-size-base` | `13px` | body text (default) |
| `--hx-font-size-lg` | `16px` | large text |
| `--hx-radius-sm` | `4px` | small radius (buttons, inputs, tags) |
| `--hx-radius-md` | `8px` | large radius (cards, modals) |
| `--hx-space-1` | `4px` | spacing scale |
| `--hx-space-2` | `8px` | spacing scale |
| `--hx-space-3` | `12px` | spacing scale |
| `--hx-space-4` | `16px` | spacing scale |
| `--hx-space-5` | `24px` | spacing scale |
| `--hx-space-6` | `32px` | spacing scale |

## Component index

All 24 components are named exports of `@cvetkov_es/react`. Every component also
accepts native HTML attributes for its host element (it extends the matching
`React.*HTMLAttributes` interface) and merges any `className` you pass with
its own `.hx-*` classes. Props shown below are only the component-specific
ones on top of that.

### Button
`ButtonProps extends React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>`
- `variant?: "primary" | "secondary" | "danger"` (default `"primary"`)
- `size?: "md" | "sm"` (default `"md"`)
```tsx
<Button variant="danger" onClick={handleDelete}>Delete</Button>
```

### Input
`InputProps extends React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>`
- `invalid?: boolean`
```tsx
<Input placeholder="Email" invalid={!isValidEmail} />
```

### Field
`FieldProps`: `{ label?: string; htmlFor?: string; className?: string; children?: React.ReactNode }`
```tsx
<Field label="Email" htmlFor="email">
  <Input id="email" />
</Field>
```

### Checkbox
`CheckboxProps extends React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>`
- `label?: string`
```tsx
<Checkbox label="I agree" checked={agreed} onChange={(e) => setAgreed(e.target.checked)} />
```

### Radio
`RadioProps extends React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>`
- `label?: string`
```tsx
<Radio name="plan" label="Pro" value="pro" checked={plan === "pro"} onChange={() => setPlan("pro")} />
```

### Toggle
`ToggleProps extends Omit<React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>, "onChange" | "checked">`
- `checked?: boolean` (default `false`)
- `onChange?: (checked: boolean) => void`
- `disabled?: boolean`
```tsx
<Toggle checked={enabled} onChange={setEnabled} />
```

### Select
`SelectProps extends React.SelectHTMLAttributes<HTMLSelectElement>`
- `invalid?: boolean`
```tsx
<Select value={country} onChange={(e) => setCountry(e.target.value)}>
  <option value="us">US</option>
  <option value="de">DE</option>
</Select>
```

### Table (+ Table.Head / Table.Row / Table.Cell / Table.HeadCell)
`TableProps extends React.TableHTMLAttributes<HTMLTableElement>`
`TableHeadProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLTableSectionElement>`
`TableRowProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLTableRowElement>`
`TableCellProps extends React.TdHTMLAttributes<HTMLTableCellElement>`
`TableHeadCellProps extends React.ThHTMLAttributes<HTMLTableCellElement>`
(no component-specific props beyond native attributes)
```tsx
<Table>
  <Table.Head>
    <Table.Row><Table.HeadCell>Name</Table.HeadCell></Table.Row>
  </Table.Head>
  <tbody>
    <Table.Row><Table.Cell>Alice</Table.Cell></Table.Row>
  </tbody>
</Table>
```

### Pagination
`PaginationProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>, "onChange">`
- `page: number`
- `pageCount: number`
- `onPageChange: (page: number) => void`
```tsx
<Pagination page={page} pageCount={10} onPageChange={setPage} />
```

### Modal
`ModalProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "title">`
- `open: boolean`
- `onClose: () => void`
- `title?: string`
```tsx
<Modal open={isOpen} onClose={() => setOpen(false)} title="Confirm">
  Are you sure?
</Modal>
```

### Drawer
`DrawerProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`
- `open: boolean`
- `onClose: () => void`
- `side?: "right" | "left"` (default `"right"`)
```tsx
<Drawer open={isOpen} onClose={() => setOpen(false)} side="right">
  Panel content
</Drawer>
```

### Tooltip
`TooltipProps`: `{ content: React.ReactNode; children: React.ReactElement }`
```tsx
<Tooltip content="Delete this item">
  <Button variant="danger">Delete</Button>
</Tooltip>
```

### Tabs
`TabsProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "onChange" | "children">`
- `value: string`
- `onChange: (value: string) => void`
- `items: TabItem[]` where `TabItem = { value: string; label: string }`
```tsx
<Tabs value={tab} onChange={setTab} items={[{ value: "a", label: "A" }, { value: "b", label: "B" }]} />
```

### Menu
`MenuProps extends Omit<React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>, "children" | "type" | "aria-haspopup" | "aria-expanded">`
- `trigger: React.ReactNode`
- `items: MenuItem[]` where `MenuItem = { label: string; onSelect: () => void }`
- `className?: string` (applies to the portalled item list, not the trigger)
```tsx
<Menu trigger={<Button>Actions</Button>} items={[{ label: "Edit", onSelect: onEdit }]} />
```

### Breadcrumbs
`BreadcrumbsProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>, "children">`
- `items: BreadcrumbItem[]` where `BreadcrumbItem = { label: string; href?: string }`
```tsx
<Breadcrumbs items={[{ label: "Home", href: "/" }, { label: "Settings" }]} />
```

### Tag
`TagProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `color?: "neutral" | "brand" | "danger"` (default `"neutral"`)
```tsx
<Tag color="brand">New</Tag>
```

### Chip
`ChipProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
- `onRemove?: () => void`
```tsx
<Chip onRemove={() => removeFilter("status")}>Status: Open</Chip>
```

### Badge
`BadgeProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>, "children">`
- `variant?: "dot" | "count" | "tag"` (default `"tag"`)
- `count?: number`
- `children?: React.ReactNode`
```tsx
<Badge variant="count" count={3} />
```

### Avatar
`AvatarProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>, "children">`
- `src?: string`
- `name: string` (required — used for alt text and initials fallback)
- `size?: "sm" | "md"` (default `"md"`)
```tsx
<Avatar name="Ada Lovelace" size="sm" />
```

### Label
`LabelProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>` (no extra props)
```tsx
<Label>Optional</Label>
```

### Alert
`AlertProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "title">`
- `severity?: "info" | "success" | "warning" | "danger"` (default `"info"`)
- `title?: string`
```tsx
<Alert severity="danger" title="Error">Something went wrong.</Alert>
```

### Icon
`IconProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>, "children">`
- `name: string` (Material Icons glyph name)
- `size?: number` (px)
```tsx
<Icon name="delete" size={16} />
```

### Calendar
`CalendarProps extends Omit<React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "onChange">`
- `value?: Date`
- `onChange: (date: Date) => void`
- `month?: Date` (controls the displayed month, independent of `value`)
```tsx
<Calendar value={date} onChange={setDate} />
```

### DatePicker
`DatePickerProps extends Omit<React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>, "value" | "onChange" | "type" | "readOnly">`
- `value?: Date`
- `onChange: (date: Date) => void`
- `placeholder?: string`
```tsx
<DatePicker value={date} onChange={setDate} placeholder="Pick a date" />
```

## Rules

DO:
- Import components from `@cvetkov_es/react` before hand-rolling any UI element —
  check this file first.
- `import "@cvetkov_es/css"` exactly once, at your app's entry point.
- Use `--hx-*` CSS variables (or the existing `.hx-*` classes) for any custom
  element that isn't covered by a component.
- Use the Roboto font stack, 13px base size, and brand color `#32B4EB` —
  i.e. `var(--hx-font-family)`, `var(--hx-font-size-base)`,
  `var(--hx-color-brand)` — rather than restating these literals.
- Load the Material Icons font yourself (aliased to family `"material"`) if
  you use `Icon`.

DON'T:
- Don't hardcode colors, fonts, radii, or spacing — reference the tokens
  above instead.
- Don't import CSS from inside library/component code — `@cvetkov_es/react`
  intentionally ships no CSS of its own so consumers can tree-shake unused
  components; only the consumer app imports `@cvetkov_es/css`, once.
- Don't `require("@cvetkov_es/react")` or `require("@cvetkov_es/css")` — both packages
  are ESM-only (`"type": "module"`, no CJS build). Use `import`.

### Good vs bad

Good — uses the component and its variant prop:
```tsx
<Button variant="danger" onClick={onDelete}>Delete</Button>
```
Bad — hand-rolled button with inline hex, ignoring the design system:
```tsx
<button style={{ background: "#ED1940", color: "#fff", borderRadius: 4 }} onClick={onDelete}>
  Delete
</button>
```

Good — a one-off custom element still styled from tokens:
```tsx
<div style={{
  padding: "var(--hx-space-3)",
  borderRadius: "var(--hx-radius-sm)",
  background: "var(--hx-color-surface-sunken)",
  color: "var(--hx-color-text-muted)",
  fontFamily: "var(--hx-font-family)",
  fontSize: "var(--hx-font-size-sm)"
}}>
  Empty state
</div>
```
Bad — same element with hardcoded values that will drift from the design system:
```tsx
<div style={{ padding: 12, borderRadius: 4, background: "#F8F8F8", color: "#777", fontFamily: "Arial", fontSize: 11 }}>
  Empty state
</div>
```
